channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Yzerman hopes new coach will revitalize Red Wings
item.description: After losing nine of their past 12 games, the Red Wings fired head coach Derek Lalonde and named Todd McLellan as his replacement.
item.dc:creator: Priya Mann, Local 4

item.title: Brighton draws 0-0 with Brentford in lackluster Premier League encounter
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Brighton had most of the chances but could not find the net in a 0-0 draw with Brentford that extended the south coast club’s winless run in the Premier League to six games on Friday. It was a frustrating night for the home side and especially Julio Enciso.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Depleted Notre Dame defense hopes to make an impact against Georgia in CFP quarterfinals
item.description: SOUTH BEND, Ind. (AP) — Notre Dame’s defense set a dominating tone early in its College Football Playoff opener against Indiana. The Fighting Irish stuffed the highest-scoring team in the CFP field for a 14-3 halftime lead, but then Notre Dame suffered a gut punch.
item.dc:creator: CURT RALLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Injured Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts won't play Sunday against Dallas
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Jalen Hurts is still in the NFL's concussion protocol, forcing the Philadelphia Eagles to play against Dallas without their star quarterback.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sarrazin to undergo surgery after head injury in crash in downhill training on 2026 Olympics slope
item.description: BORMIO, Italy (AP) — French standout Cyprien Sarrazin was in intensive care with a head injury and will undergo surgery after a crash during training for a World Cup downhill race on Friday on the slope set to be used for the 2026 Winter Olympics.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls take on the Bucks on 3-game skid
item.description: Milwaukee Bucks (16-13, fifth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (13-18, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: Chicago enters the matchup against Milwaukee after losing three straight games. The Bulls are 2-5 against division opponents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Breaking: Dave Fleming leaves CMU after 20 years
item.description: "I thank Coach Fleming for all his dedication and hard work at CMU," Colorado Mesa Athletic Director Joan McDermott said in a press release. "He is not only a great coach, but a special person and will be greatly missed.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, KREX 5

item.title: Blackhawks drop 2nd straight on road trip in 4-3 loss to Wild
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Brock Faber snapped a tie in the third period, and the Minnesota Wild stopped a four-game losing streak by edging the Blackhawks, 4-3, on Monday night. Kirill Kaprizov, Jared Spurgeon and Marcus Foligno also scored for Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Shohei Ohtani wins 3rd AP Male Athlete of the Year award, tying Michael Jordan for 1 shy of record
item.description: The Japanese superstar caps 2024 by winning The Associated Press Male Athlete of the Year for the third time, tying him with basketball great Michael Jordan. He trails only four-time winners Lance Armstrong, Tiger Woods and LeBron James.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: Tiger Woods never had so much fun losing in a playoff. For the 67-year-old Bernhard Langer, it never gets old winning the PNC Championship with his son.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Penix wins debut as Atlanta QB as Falcons return two picks for TDs, beat hapless Giants 34-7
item.description: Jessie Bates and Matthew Judon each had a pick-6 to carry Atlanta (8-7) to its second straight win after a four-game losing streak put its postseason hopes in jeopardy.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs' Mayfield loses key member of offense before his Texas homecoming
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers have another difficult road test in their attempt to win the NFC South title.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the Yankees agree to a $12.5 million, 1-year deal, AP source says
item.description: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the New York Yankees agreed Saturday to a $12.5 million, one-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Winter Classic Day 3: Fruita bounces back, GJ Girls win thriller
item.description: Another loaded, action-packed day of hoops at the 2024 Family Health West Winter Classic is in the books.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Caprock Hockey hangs tough with defending State Champion Durango
item.description: In their second-ever varsity home game, the Caprock Eagles once again had a tough test, this time with the defending 4A State Champion Durango Demons.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
